rule: Record DOI/URL, route family, source type, material simulant or regolith domain, energy boundary, batch mass, loss multiplier, site-time evidence, structural object and claim wording.
reason: Makes LC-ERL assignments auditable from public evidence rather than narrative judgement.

rule: Source signal only; no route ranking or feasibility wording.
reason: Prevents extraction candidates from becoming engineering claims.

rule: Registry/context evidence with traceable source metadata but no comparable route-volume-power row.
reason: Separates source inclusion from quantitative screening.

rule: Descriptor-level route screen with a bounded comparator variable and declared material-domain boundary.
reason: Allows comparison while blocking site-specific route recommendation.

rule: Evidence-gated diagnostic assignment with allowed wording, forbidden wording and named blocked gates.
reason: Supports measurement prioritization without claiming route closure.

rule: Route-complete engineering evidence under a declared site, object, processed mass, power and loss boundary.
reason: Defines the minimum threshold for engineering closure.

rule: Mission-admissible claim for a specified envelope with mission-analogue or field-demonstrated evidence.
reason: Prevents local or laboratory evidence from becoming universal deployment language.

rule: Use public measured values first; modelled or inferred values remain bounded scenarios; private or unpublished quantities are not inserted as facts.
reason: Protects reference integrity and cold-sinter batch-boundary treatment.

rule: A missing necessary gate is coded as blocked or partial, never imputed as closed.
reason: Makes evidence absence visible to reviewers and designers.

rule: LC-ERL is a claim-readiness and claim-wording framework, not an official technology readiness level, procurement score or mission certification.
reason: Keeps the framework inside the evidence actually supplied.
